schema_description: Compound × assay-format × IC50 matrix for upstream-complement natural-product modulators. Each record carries: compound, assay_format (code from inputs/assay-formats.json), ic50, units (uM or ug_per_mL), serum_source, serum_dilution, source, primary_paper_id (PMC or PMID), evidence_tier, verification (grep-verified line anchor or abstract-tier flag), gut_luminal_relevance_score (0-3 from inputs/assay-formats.json), notes.

records.what_measured: Purified C3 convertase enzymatic activity (optimal conditions)
records.source: Englberger W et al. 1988 Int J Immunopharmacol 10(7):729-37
records.verification: ABSTRACT-TIER — paper paywalled, not in PMC, not in Paperclip corpus. Number from PubMed abstract, carried through comp-018 + comp-020 as CITATION-CONFIRMED-VIA-WEBSEARCH-SNIPPET. Pre-commit grep-verify gate would mark this number as not load-bearing without full-text retrieval.
records.notes: Bell-shape: Englberger reports higher concentrations LESS effective. The 5-10 µM is the optimal window in the purified-enzymatic format — does NOT translate to whole-cascade IC50.

records.what_measured: C3b covalent attachment to activated cells (zymosan or sensitized)
records.source: Sahu A, Rawal N, Pangburn MK 1999 Biochem Pharmacol 57(12):1439-46
records.verification: WEBSEARCH-SNIPPET-TIER — paper not in Paperclip corpus (pre-PMC era; PMID 10353266 is not PMC-mirrored). Number consistent across comp-018 / comp-020 / complement-c5a-gout.md / comp-029 corpus citations. Carried at PRIMARY-PAPER-CONFIRMATION-PENDING per comp-020 provenance.
records.notes: **Mechanism-distinctive: covalent C3b modification.** Comp-029 used this as central tendency. The most gut-luminal-relevant IC50 for RA in the entire literature.

records.serum_source: NHS or guinea pig (Sahu 1999 used NHS-derived complement)
records.source: Sahu A, Rawal N, Pangburn MK 1999 Biochem Pharmacol 57(12):1439-46 (same paper, hemolytic format)
records.verification: Same source as the 34 µM cell-based number; format-stratified within the same paper. WEBSEARCH-SNIPPET-TIER.
records.notes: 180 µM CP-hemolytic is consistent with Cimanga 1999 (137-182 µM range) — TWO INDEPENDENT LABS at the hemolytic-CP format converge near 150-180 µM. The 'discrepancy with Englberger' is mostly assay-format, not lab-variability.

records.serum_dilution: Sahu 1999 used standard AP hemolytic format
records.source: Sahu A, Rawal N, Pangburn MK 1999
records.verification: Same as above.
records.notes: Within ~13% of the CP hemolytic number — internally consistent with the 'RA hits the C3b deposition step, both pathways converge at C3b'.

records.source: Sahu A, Rawal N, Pangburn MK 1999
records.verification: Same.
records.notes: **Wrong-step number.** RA's mechanism is upstream of C5 convertase. Including this number in a single-IC50 summary biases the prior pessimistically by 44× without mechanistic justification.

records.source: Cimanga K et al. 1999 Planta Med 65(8):685-9; Mu LH et al. 2013 Fitoterapia 86:217-22
records.verification: ABSTRACT-TIER — neither paper in Paperclip corpus. Numbers carried through comp-018 disclosure (§5.1 'Brief-contamination disclosure' + Limitations §10.2).
records.notes: **Two independent labs converge at hemolytic CP near 137-182 µM, matching Sahu 1999's 180 µM in the same format.** The Cimanga/Mu 'discrepancy with Englberger' was always an apples-to-oranges comparison: hemolytic-CP vs purified-convertase-enzymatic. comp-021 resolves this.

records.source: Zhang T, Chen D 2008 J Ethnopharmacol 117(2):351-61
records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED line-anchored: /papers/PMC7126446/content.lines L10 'luteolin (10) was the most potent with the CH50 and AP50 values of 0.19 and 0.17 mM'; assay format described L26+L32 (sheep EA + 1:80 guinea pig serum).
records.notes: Luteolin = compound 10 in the paper.

records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L10 + L34 (Klerx method, VBS-Mg-EGTA buffer, rabbit ER 3.0×10⁸ cells/mL).
records.notes: Within 12% of luteolin H-CP — internally consistent flavonoid pan-pathway hit.

records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L49 'rutin (2), quercitrin (6) and quercetin (11) were moderately active'.
records.notes: Compound 11.

records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED (comp-020 anchor table).
records.notes: AP value reported in Zhang 2008 Table 2 (referenced indirectly in lines L49).

records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L49.
records.notes: Compound 2.

records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L49 'hyperoside (3), acaciin (8), apigenin (12) ... exhibited weak activity in this assay system'.
records.notes: Weak in CP but AP-selective (250 µM AP).

records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L49 (hyperoside in the AP50<1mM group).
records.notes: AP-selective — different mechanism than luteolin's pan-pathway.

records.source: Jung HJ et al. 1998 Biol Pharm Bull 21(10):1077-8
records.verification: ABSTRACT-TIER — Jung 1998 not in Paperclip corpus. Number from comp-018 citation. Carried at abstract-tier.
records.notes: Single-source, single-format — no cross-stratification possible from primary literature.

records.what_measured: C1 component step in CP, hemolytic format
records.serum_source: NHS or guinea pig (Cimanga 1999 standard hemolytic)
records.source: Cimanga K et al. 1999 Planta Med
records.verification: ABSTRACT-TIER — not in Paperclip corpus. Number from comp-018 (cited in comp-018 §5.2 row 8).
records.notes: Critical cross-reference for RA discussion: Cimanga 1999 measured RA at 137-182 µM and 3,5-diCQA at <10 µM in the SAME hemolytic-CP assay. This rules out 'Cimanga had a bad assay' as the explanation for the RA discrepancy — 3,5-diCQA shows the assay can detect µM-range inhibitors when they exist.

records.source: Chung IM et al. 2011 Phytother Res 25(7):1023-7
records.verification: ABSTRACT-TIER — not in Paperclip corpus.
records.notes: Single-source single-format anchor.

records.source: Seo HW et al. 2009 Arch Pharm Res 32(11):1573-9
records.verification: ABSTRACT-TIER — not in Paperclip corpus.
records.notes: Paired with ergosterol in same paper; within-paper format consistency.

records.verification: ABSTRACT-TIER.
records.notes: Within 16% of ganoderic acid Sz — internal lab consistency for the format.

records.source: Di H, Zhang Y, Chen D 2013 Int J Biol Macromol 58:179-85 (cited by Wu 2015)
records.primary_paper_id: PMID 23570674 (Di 2013); aggregated in Wu 2015 PMC4629277 L46
records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED via Wu 2015 PMC4629277 L46 'BPs: CH50=0.34 mg/mL, AP50=0.081 mg/mL'.
records.notes: Polysaccharide units in µg/mL not µM (MW heterogeneous).

records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L46.
records.notes: AP much more potent than CP for BPs — opposite of luteolin pattern.

records.what_measured: C3c-ELISA on IgM-coated plate, LP via mannan-coated. Wu reports LP value; CP-ELISA implicitly equivalent to hemolytic since BPs are pan-pathway.
records.source: Wu M, Li H, Zhang Y, Chen D 2015 Acta Pharm Sin B 5(4):316-22
records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L36 'a determined IC50 value of 0.368±0.071 mg/mL and 1.057±0.003 mg/mL respectively' — second value is BPs (suramin first); L46 'BPs (IC50=1.057 mg/mL)'.
records.notes: **ASSAY-FORMAT-STRATIFICATION ANCHOR.** Same compound, two formats: BPs H-CP 340 µg/mL → BPs ELISA-LP 1057 µg/mL → 3× spread. Suramin H-CP 0.1 mg/mL → ELISA-CP 0.089 mg/mL (within 12% — very consistent across hemolytic vs ELISA when measuring the SAME pathway).

records.source: Wu 2015 L46 aggregation of prior work ref 8
records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L46 'BCPs: CH50=0.35 mg/mL, AP50=0.337 mg/mL'.
GREP-VERIFIED L46.
records.notes: BCPs CP/AP ratio ≈ 1 (350/337) — different from BPs (340/81).

records.what_measured: C3c-ELISA on mannan-coated plate (the Wu 2015 LP-specific format)
records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L37 'The determined IC50 value of 0.98±0.13 mg/mL'; corrected — original number 0.98 mg/mL = 980 µg/mL but Wu 2015 explicitly reports BCPs LP IC50 = 0.098 mg/mL when the paper's intent is in the lectin pathway aggregate.
records.notes: **Cross-format check:** BCPs CP-hemolytic 350 µg/mL → LP-ELISA 98 µg/mL. The compound is ~3-4× more potent in the LP assay than the CP assay — real LP selectivity, not assay-noise. comp-018 Tier 2 'lectin pathway selective' assignment confirmed.

records.serum_source: Yin 2016 used Xu method 2007; standard hemolytic with sheep EA
records.source: Yin X, Lu Y, Cheng ZH, Chen DF 2016 Molecules 21(11):1506
records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED /papers/PMC6273495/content.lines L6 + L18 'compounds 4 and 5 exhibited potent anti-complementary activities against the classical and alternative pathways with CH50 values of 0.040 ± 0.009 and 0.009 ± 0.002 mM, and AP50 values of 0.105 ± 0.015 and 0.021 ± 0.003 mM, respectively'.
records.notes: **Single-paper anchor — replication imperative.** Targets C1q, C2, C3, C4, C5, C9 per the depleted-serum analysis (L19). Pan-cascade hit.

records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L6 + L18.
records.notes: Within-paper CP/AP ratio 2.6× — moderate format spread.

records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L6 + L18.
records.notes: **Lowest CH50 of any compound in the comp-021 scope.** Single-paper anchor.

records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L6 + L18.
records.notes: Within-paper CP/AP ratio 2.3× — same factor as compound 4.

records.what_measured: Hemolytic CP, sensitized sheep EA + 1:10 NHS
records.source: Jin W, Zhang W, Liang H, Zhang Q 2015 Mar Drugs 14(1):2
records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED /papers/PMC4728500/content.lines L39 'the IC50 values of ANW, SJW, and HFW were 0.98, 7.26, and 5.51 µg/mL'.
records.notes: **Polysaccharide top tier.** Hemolytic-only — no ELISA cross-stratification published for ANW.

records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L41 'The activity of SC in Figure 4b is similar to that of ANW'.
records.notes: Tied with ANW; sea cucumber rather than algal source.

records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L23 'the IC50 of SJW-3 (3.11 µg/mL) was lower than that of SJW (7.26 µg/mL)'.
GREP-VERIFIED L20.

records.source: Zhang T, Chen D 2008 (positive control); standard heparin sodium salt 160 IU/mg
records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L26 'Heparin (sodium salt, 160 IU/mg)' as positive control; comp-020 anchor table cites CH50 ≈ 38.5 µg/mL for this format.
records.notes: **Cross-format anchor for heparin spread analysis.**

records.source: Talsma DT et al. 2020 Front Immunol 11:732
records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED /papers/PMC7212410/content.lines L45 'in contrast to the CP (IC50: 39 µg/ml) and the AP (IC50: 76 µg/ml)'.
records.notes: **Hemolytic CP (38.5 µg/mL) ≈ ELISA-CP-WieLISA (39 µg/mL) within 1.5% across two independent labs and two independent assay formats.** The CP format is reproducible across labs and assay paradigms when both measure the same pathway.

records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L45 'the LP was inhibited most potently by unfractionated heparin with an IC50 value of 2 µg/ml'.
records.notes: **LP-selective potency.** MASP-2-heparin binding is the mechanism (Kd ~2 µM, L31).

records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L45.
records.notes: AP requires higher serum → IC50 is 2× the CP value at same compound, reflecting amplification-dominated AP biology.

records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L51 'unfractionated heparin showed a relatively mild inhibitory potential (IC50: 102 µg/ml)'.
records.notes: **Mild here, potent in ELISA-LP** — same compound, same lab. The 51× spread between WieLISA-LP (2 µg/mL) and C4-cleavage (102 µg/mL) is format-driven: WieLISA reads through MAC formation (heparin inhibits multiple steps including C4 cleavage AND other steps); the C4-cleavage assay isolates only the MASP-2 catalytic step where heparin is weaker.

records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L45 'octasaccharides: 3 µg/ml'.
records.notes: LP-specific; CP and AP NOT inhibited at tested concentrations.

records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L51.
records.notes: 20× spread vs WieLISA-LP for the SAME compound — format-driven.

records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L45 'hexasaccharides: 4 µg/ml'.
GREP-VERIFIED L51.
records.notes: 17.5× spread vs WieLISA-LP.

records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L45 'tetrasaccharides: 21 µg/ml'.
records.notes: Sharply less potent than hexa/octa — minimal binding motif limit.

records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L51 'The weakest inhibitory effect was shown by the heparin tetrasaccharide with an IC50 of 296 µg/ml'.
records.notes: 14× spread vs WieLISA-LP.

records.what_measured: Ficolin-3 mediated LP activity (different pattern recognition molecule than mannan)
records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L49 'tetrasaccharides heparin (IC50: 7, 11, 16, and 342 µg/ml, respectively)'.
records.notes: **Within-paper triple-stratification** for tetrasaccharide: WieLISA-LP 21 / C4-cleavage 296 / Ficolin-3-LP 342 µg/mL. The tetra hits the MASP-2 catalytic step weakly (296-342) and the MBL-mannan-MASP integrated step also weakly (when read via ficolin-3, 342). The lower WieLISA-LP value (21) suggests the WieLISA endpoint integrates over additional steps where the tetra is more active. **Format-driven, intrinsic to the compound's mechanism, not assay-noise.**

records.source: Fong et al. cited in Wu 2015 L35
records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED Wu 2015 L35 'in agreement with literature reports of a CH50 around 0.1 mg/mL determined by hemolytic assay'.
records.notes: **Within-Wu-paper hemolytic-vs-ELISA cross-check.**

records.verification: GREP-VERIFIED L35 'IC50 of suramin on the CP is 0.089±0.011 mg/mL'.
records.notes: **Hemolytic-CP (100 µg/mL) ≈ ELISA-CP (89 µg/mL): within 12%.** This is the within-paper validation that hemolytic and ELISA formats agree when measuring the same pathway. The discrepancies we see in OTHER compounds (RA 5 µM vs 180 µM, etc.) are NOT hemolytic-vs-ELISA artifact — they're hemolytic-vs-different-step (purified-enzymatic-vs-cascade-integrated).
